_provenance.scope: descriptive — machine-readable mirror of DESIGN.md; hand-authored from BRAND.md as the proof-of-concept output of /stardust:seed
notes: no source site exists; this is the greenfield-seeded equivalent of an extract artifact
all colors lifted verbatim from BRAND.md palette block
typography stack is intent-only; final faces selected during prototyping
voice.examples lifted from BRAND.md voice anchors

name: Holler & Hymn
description: Single-maker Appalachian botanical-liqueur house — Wolfpen Holler, Madison County NC

chip-accent.use: irreverence accent — one per viewport maximum

patterns.name: two-register-alternation
patterns.evidence: every section commits to bulletin (cream) or revival (hymnal-black); scroll alternates the two

patterns.name: hand-painted-bottle-card
patterns.evidence: the bottles page and home featured products render each product as a hand-painted label artifact on a cream card

patterns.name: all-caps-display
patterns.evidence: every display headline is hand-lettered all-caps; title case is forbidden for display

patterns.name: typewriter-numerals
patterns.evidence: ABV, dates, phone, addresses, coordinates all set in IBM-Plex-Mono — literalism as visual claim

patterns.name: hand-painted-ornament
patterns.evidence: SVG marks (crossed sourwood, revival tent silhouette, copper still, ampersand) at section breaks; no two identical

patterns.name: single-page-scroll-narrative
patterns.evidence: home is a long-scroll digest; deep pages expand each section

patterns.name: no-shadow-flat-system
patterns.evidence: no element carries a drop shadow; depth is in the painted-label imagery only

do: Numbers and proper nouns (forty miles, 2019, twenty-seven herbs, Wolfpen Holler, the preacher's shed).
Sustained but sparing wordplay on revival/gospel/sin/salvation vocabulary.
dont: We / our team / passionate / crafted / curated / purveyors / lifestyle / artisanal.
Adjective stacks of three or more.
Apostrophes in product names trying to sound country (Granny's, Daddy's).

do: Saturday night sin. Sunday morning hymn. Both in one bottle.
Made by hand. From a to z.
Twenty-seven herbs, eleven labels, one preacher's shed.
If you find one in a bar in Asheville, the bartender probably knows me.
I'll pour four spirits. I'll tell you about each one.
dont: Our passionate team of artisans crafts each bottle with care.
An aromatic compound profile of vanilla orchid and crushed velvet.

rules: Voice cheek lives in product names and label art, not in Wendell's narration.
Sacred/profane wordplay is restrained — each instance must land; never every section.
Numbers prove literalism; use them in place of adjectives.
First-person singular everywhere except the legal/utility footer.

systemComponents.name: site-header
systemComponents.anchor: bulletin-cream ground, hand-painted H&H monogram left, typewriter-mono nav links, single barn-rust 'Visit' CTA right

systemComponents.name: site-footer
systemComponents.anchor: hymnal-black ground, three columns (direct contact / site links / legal), typewriter-mono throughout

systemComponents.name: age-gate-modal
systemComponents.anchor: loads before any page content; revival-tent mode; two buttons (yes / no), no dismissable banner pattern

systemComponents.name: two-register-section
systemComponents.anchor: alternating bulletin / revival mode sections; the scroll rhythm

systemComponents.name: find-a-pew-band
systemComponents.anchor: revival-tent ground, display headline, barn-rust CTA
